1.11a: Pre-completion queries (non-Limited Company BTL): LMS Secure Link portal via Conveyancer Zone.
General address: Mortgage Lending Department, 26 Sovereign Street, Leeds, LS1 4BJ.
1.11b: Pre-completion queries (non-Limited Company BTL): LMS Secure Link portal via Conveyancer Zone.
General address: Mortgage Lending Department, 26 Sovereign Street, Leeds, LS1 4BJ.
1.7: Lending where borrower and mortgagor are not the same is not permitted.
1.14: Firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: Firm may act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower, provided a different person handles the conveyance.
No conflict of interest must exist or arise.
Law Society guidelines must be followed.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's solicitor details is required only if they are not in a legal directory or not registered with the SRA or CLC at the provided address.
3.1.4: Lending is not permitted if the seller has no legal representation.
3.1.5: Primary ID (one required): UK/EU/EEA/Swiss Passport, Photo Driving Licence (full/provisional), Biometric Residence Card (not permits), EU/EEA Identity Card, NI Voter's Card, Blue Disabled Driver's Pass, Shotgun/Firearms Certificate.

Secondary ID (two required): Paper DL (pre-1998), Birth Certificate (under 18 savings only), Benefits notification, HMRC Tax notification (not P45/P60), UK/EU regulated firm bank/mortgage statement, Council Tax bill, Utility bill, Court instrument, Personal cheque, Solicitor letter.

Address Verification (one required): DL with address, Council Tax bill, Benefit letter, HMRC notification, Utility bill (last 3 months, no internet prints), Mortgage statement (last 12 months), LEA grant/loan agreement, Council rent card/tenancy agreement, Postal bank statement (last 3 months), Credit/Debit card statement (last 3 months, no internet prints), Solicitor letter (last 3 months).
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor details is required only if they are not in a legal directory or not registered with the SRA or CLC at the provided address.
3.2.4: Lending is not permitted if the seller has no legal representation.
4.1: Valuation report copy will be provided.
Conveyancer must review valuer comments directed at legal advisors.
4.3: Contact Mortgage Lending Department (Section 1.11a) if valuer assumptions are incorrect.
4.5a: Contact details for re-inspection are as per Section 1.11a.
4.5b: Certificate of Title should be sent to the contact point in Section 1.11a.
5.1.1: Refer all cases where the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months to the Mortgage Lending Department.
Sub-sales, sale and leasebacks, or back-to-back transactions are normally not permitted.
Provide: confirmation of current ownership, date of acquisition, and purchase price paid by borrower (or acquisition date and price for remortgages).
Assignable contracts and irrevocable Powers of Attorney in favor of intervening sellers are not acceptable.
5.2.1: Contact details for non-owner sellers are as per Section 1.11a.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required by the lender.
Japanese Knotweed requires a 10-year property-specific, transferable guarantee.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if compliant with the Search Code, backed by adequate PI insurance, and meeting Part 1 paragraph 5.4.7 requirements.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to Part 1 paragraph 5.4.7 and providing an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Report planning/condition breaches to the contact point in Section 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required but must be retained on file.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents are not required but must be retained on file.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required but must be retained on file.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting marketability to Section 1.11a.
Acceptable Section 106 restrictions are defined in the Society's specific online Solicitors Instructions.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are permitted up to 15% of the property area.
Flying freeholds exceeding 15% must be referred to the Mortgage Lending Department.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.7.1c: Report flying freehold or freehold flat matters to Section 1.11a.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into max 4 flats where borrower owns/occupies one and owns the freehold are acceptable.
Security must include the leasehold flat and the freehold title.
5.8.5: Cross-over freeholds/leases on two-flat buildings: Mortgage required on borrower's leasehold interest.
If possession is taken, share of freehold must transfer simultaneously; trust deed required if not in lease.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to the Mortgage Lending Department (Section 1.11a).
5.13.1: Gifted deposits must be from family members only.
Gifts must be non-repayable, from UK residents with UK bank accounts, and require a declaration of amount.
Bankruptcy searches required for all parties contributing to the deposit.
Ltd Co BTL: Deposits accepted from directors or shareholders; family gifted deposits not permitted; director loans are acceptable.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 85 years from mortgage commencement.
5.14.9: Pre-June 30 2022 leases: Ground rent must not exceed 0.1% of property value.
Pre-June 30 2022 leases: Rent must not double more frequently than every 25 years (e.g., 5, 10, 15 year doubling is unacceptable).
On/After June 30 2022 leases: Ground rent must be peppercorn.
Lease structure: Must ensure borrower's lease survives any forfeiture of superior leases.
5.14.10: Contact point for service charge matters is Section 1.11a.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if an unqualified CoT can be given.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts are not required but must be retained on file.
5.14.13: Evidence of service of notice is not required but must be retained on file.
5.14.15a: Contact point for absentee/insolvent landlords is Section 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee/insolvent landlords is accepted if an unqualified CoT can be given.
5.14.17a: Contact the Society if LPE1 Form questions 8.19 or 8.20 provide/promise building safety documents.
5.14.17b: Building safety checks are not required unless specific documentation is identified via LPE1 Form.
5.14.17c: Remortgage building safety requirements follow the same protocol as standard purchases.
5.15.2a: Estate Rentcharges are acceptable if: Section 121 LPA is excluded; OR 28 days notice to mortgagee is required; OR owner is a residents' management company.
Historic rentcharges (uncollected for 12+ years) require indemnity insurance.
Max Rentcharge amount: 0.2% of property value; must not more than double every 25 years.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required but must be retained on file.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required but must be retained on file.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and Articles of Association are not required but must be retained on file.
5.16.2: Notify the lender only if clear bankruptcy searches cannot be obtained.
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney: Upload to LMS STARS post-completion.
Ltd Co BTL PoA: Email to LBSgeneraladmin@peppergroup.co.uk.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of PoA is not required but must be retained on file.
5.19.1: Affordable housing/Shared Equity: Must comply with online 'Solicitors Instructions' and upload docs to LMS STARS.
5.20.1: Solar PV Panel leases must meet UK Finance minimums and the Society's 'Solar Panel Pack'.
5.20.3: Solar PV leases must have a mortgagee break clause exercisable on possession.
Provider is responsible for removal costs and making good damage.
Building insurer must be notified of the installation.
5.20.4: Disclosure of Green Deal Plans is required via Section 1.11a.
6.1.3: Contact Section 1.11a if borrower does not proceed.
6.2.1: Contact Section 1.11a for property description discrepancies.
6.3.1: Contact Section 1.11a for issues relating to purchase price.
6.4.4: Report financial incentives >5% of value/price, part-exchanges, or finders fees to Section 1.11a using UK Finance DIF.
Incentives are not permitted if LTV >90%.
Certificate of Title must not be submitted until the Disclosure Form is sent.
6.4.5: Report lack of control over purchase money to Section 1.11a.
6.5.1: Report lack of vacant possession to Section 1.11a.
6.6.1: Lending on buy-to-let is permitted; contact Section 1.11a.
6.6.2: Report missing letting details to Section 1.11a.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements are not required but must be retained on file.
6.6.4: HMO lending is permitted; require completed HMO License or Council exemption confirmation.
6.7.1: Accepted Warranties: ABC+, Advantage HCI, AEDIS, Ark, Build Assure, BLP, Build Zone, Castle 10, Global Home Warranties, ICW, LABC, NHBC, One Guarantee, Premier, Protek, Q Policy, Zurich Municipal (pre-Oct 2009).
PCC accepted from: CIAT, RIBA, RICS, MICE, MIStructE with PI evidence.
6.7.2: Send certified copy of warranty Policy and Certificate.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes are not required but must be retained on file.
6.7.4: Will proceed without warranty if: PCC (per 6.7.1) provided, Architects certificate for borrower benefit exists, Building Regs sign-off obtained, and PI Insurance exists.
6.7.6: Professional Consultant's Certificate is not required to be sent but must be retained on file.
6.8.1: Contact Section 1.11a for issues with unadopted roads or sewers.
6.9.1: Contact Section 1.11a if necessary easements are absent.
6.10.2: Retentions and instalments are released directly to the borrower.
6.11.1: Contact Section 1.11a for redevelopment or road proposals.
6.12.1: Contact Section 1.11a for pre-emption rights or resale restrictions.
7.3: Standard Declaration by Occupier required for all occupants aged 17+.
Ltd Co BTL: Occupier consent not required; borrower must comply with tenancy conditions.
8.1: Advising specified third parties is not permitted.
9.1: Indemnity policies are not required to be sent but must be retained on file.
9.2: Minimum indemnity limit: Mortgage advance plus 10%.
10.2a: Advance paid via CHAPS; bank details must be in CoT.
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance: 5 working days.
10.3: Accounts starting with 'L' or Limited Company BTL: Funds transfer fee deducted from advance.
Accounts with 'Mortgage Application Number': No deductions; fees paid in advance or added to loan.
10.7: Funds must be returned via CHAPS if completion delayed >1 working day.
App Number accounts: HSBC, Sort Code 40-64-38, Account: First 8 digits of Application Number.
Other/BTL accounts: Call 03458 480061 for bank details.
10.9: Contact Section 1.11a if completion is delayed.
10.10: Funds can be held for maximum 1 working day before return is required.
10.11: Interest charged at contractual rate from date of transfer until funds are returned or completion occurs.
12.3.1: Contact Section 1.11a for release of retentions/instalments.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deed should be retained on file (send to HMLR or keep if using e-DRS).

Delay updates required every 30 days via LMS if registration not complete.
